# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder:14b)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that creates releases and publishes packages. On version tags, build the application, create a GitHub release, and publish to npm/PyPI. Make the release process fully automated so developers can just push tags to trigger releases.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Release Workflow

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - 'v*'

jobs:
  build-and-release:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout@v2

    - name: Set up Node.js (for npm)
      if: github.repository.endsWith('.js')
      uses: actions/setup-node@v2
      with:
        node-version: '14'

    - name: Set up Python (for PyPI)
      if: github.repository.endsWith('.py')
      uses: actions/setup-python@v2
      with:
        python-version: '3.8'

    - name: Install dependencies
      run: |
        npm install  # for npm packages
        pip install setuptools wheel twine  # for Python packages

    - name: Build application
      run: npm run build  # adjust this command as necessary

    - name: Create release on GitHub
      uses: actions/create-release@v1
      env:
        GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
      with:
        tag_name: ${{ github.ref }}
        release_name: Release ${{ github.ref }}
        body: |
          Changes in this release:

          - Description of changes

    - name: Publish to npm
      if: github.repository.endsWith('.js')
      run: |
        npm config set //registry.npmjs.org/:_authToken=${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}
        npm publish

    - name: Publish to PyPI
      if: github.repository.endsWith('.py')
      run: |
        python setup.py sdist bdist_wheel
        twine upload dist/* --username __token__ --password ${{ secrets.PYPI_API_TOKEN }}
